Q38: My family and I have been fishing at Harlan County Reservoir for over 50 years.  We have primarily targeted Wipers there since their introduction approximately 30 years ago.  I think Wipers have greatly benefitted the fishery, and I continue to be frustrated by the well-funded and organized walleye fisherman at Harlan despite studies (including the 3 year Kansas State biologist study) that show wipers do not affect the walleye fishery.  Recently, I've read and heard hints that make me wonder if the NPGC is placating the walleye group as wiper stocking levels and frequency have been reduced.  It's frustrating that this well-organized walleye group could be getting undue influence on the Harlan fishery. I fished Harlan over 150 days this year, and it was obvious that between late June and the end of September, that over 75% of the fisherman were targeting wipers and white bass, with large wipers being the primary goal.  The continued reduction or elimination of wipers from Harlan, would greatly hurt the local economy.  I also believe that wipers are vital to the health of the Harlan fishery by controlling the shad population.
